name: TC-SF-005 — GET /api/v1/fetch/status: checkedAt is a recent ISO 8601 timestamp (< 60 s old)
description:
**What this test does:**
Checks that checkedAt is:
1. A parseable ISO 8601 datetime
2. Not in the future
3. No more than 60 seconds old

**Why it matters:** If checkedAt is stale (older than 60 s), the status response is a cached snapshot from a previous check, not a live reading. Operators relying on this endpoint for real-time health decisions would be looking at outdated data.

A freshness window of 60 seconds is reasonable for a service that checks itself on every request or on a short poll interval.
descriptionHtml:
What this test does:
Checks that checkedAt is:

Why it matters: If checkedAt is stale (older than 60 s), the status response is a cached snapshot from a previous check, not a live reading. Operators relying on this endpoint for real-time health decisions would be looking at outdated data.

A freshness window of 60 seconds is reasonable for a service that checks itself on every request or on a short poll interval.
